title: salix × eriocataphylla
intro: Salix × eriocataphylla is a hybrid willow plant, a cross between the weeping willow (Salix babylonica) and the goat willow (Salix caprea), known for its distinctive white or pink catkins. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been used to treat various health issues such as skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Salix × eriocataphylla include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
